qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Columnar upload metadata endpoint allows low-privilege memory-exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a dataset SQL-overwrite authorization bypass for low-privileged users, while this finding is a ZIP inflation availability issue in the upload-metadata columnar parsing path. The endpoint, vulnerable code, missing guard, and consequence are all different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described improper authorization bug in dataset update logic. The only qualifying finding is a separate authenticated upload-metadata denial-of-service issue.
evidence: The advisory says a low-privileged authenticated user with dataset-write and chart-read permissions can bypass data access controls by overwriting the SQL query of an existing dataset.

The only qualifying finding targets POST /api/v1/database/upload_metadata/ and attributes exploitation to unbounded ZIP member inflation in the columnar metadata reader before Parquet metadata extraction.
The finding does not involve dataset SQL overwrite logic, existing-dataset update behavior, chart-read permissions, or bypass of dataset authorization checks.
